footnotes.text: A hundred and forty-four thousand: these are the faithful followers of the Lamb, for they have his name and that of the Father written on their foreheads. One of their characteristics is purity; they are virgins. Cf. 14, 4.
Babylon: in Jewish and Christian circles, Babylon was a synonym for Rome.
The figure of the wheat harvest seems to refer to the good; the figure of the vintage in v. 19 refers to the bad.
A thousand and six hundred stadia: a considerable distance, but the number is symbolical and signifies a judgment that is complete and final, and reaching to all corners of the earth.
